meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing Strategies to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Overcome project stagnation by mastering SaaS pricing models. A high-level guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship profitable apps.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing random ideas. Use SaaS pricing models as your product blueprint. By reverse-engineering high-value billing tiers, you define core features before writing a single line of code. This framework turns abstract concepts into revenue-generating assets, ensuring your client projects are always commercially viable from day one.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambition. You've been staring at the screen, paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' trap, while your billable hours evaporate. The truth is, you aren't lacking creativity—you're lacking a commercial North Star. When you treat SaaS pricing as your primary architectural constraint, the product features reveal themselves naturally.

This is how elite freelancers ship. Instead of guessing what to build, we model the revenue flow, define the value metrics, and use AI-agentic workflows to materialize the stack. By the time we start coding, we aren't guessing; we are executing a validated business model.

steps.title: Define the Revenue Model
steps.description: Identify the primary value metric (e.g., seats, API calls, or storage) to dictate your database schema and feature requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze 5 competitor pricing pages in the [Niche] space. Extract their value metrics and core feature tiers into a structured JSON schema suitable for a SaaS billing backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a schema.prisma file based on the pricing tier logic extracted from the analysis.'

steps.title: Architect the Subscription Logic
steps.description: Create a robust billing state machine that handles plan transitions, preventing edge-case bugs in user access control.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust state machine for a Stripe-integrated SaaS. Handle 'trialing', 'active', 'past_due', and 'canceled' states with clear transition rules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Implement the billing webhook handlers in a new file named billing.ts using the generated state machine.'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI Component
steps.description: Develop a high-conversion pricing component that dynamically updates based on the database schema defined in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive React pricing table component using Tailwind CSS. Must include a toggle for monthly vs. yearly billing and highlight the 'Pro' tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a PricingTable.tsx component and integrate it into the landing page.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Use middleware to enforce access controls based on the user's active subscription tier, ensuring security by default.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware function for Next.js that checks user subscription status against a required feature flag before allowing route access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Inject the subscription check middleware into the application router.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment and Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to verify that your billing logic holds under simulated user scenarios.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a set of end-to-end integration tests using Playwright that simulate a user upgrading their plan and gaining access to a gated feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Run all tests and provide a summary of potential edge cases in the billing flow.'

pro_tips: Always build your database schema starting from the 'Billing' table; it forces you to define user roles before you build the UI.
Use 'Feature Flags' as a service to decouple deployment from release, allowing you to test pricing tiers with real users without redeploying code.
Ignore 'Free' tiers for your first iteration; focus exclusively on the 'Pro' tier to ensure the core value proposition is actually worth paying for.
Implement 'Usage-based' triggers in your backend early, even if you start with flat pricing, to allow for future-proof scaling.
Use Claude Code to perform 'Red Teaming' on your own billing logic—ask it to find ways to bypass access controls.
content.next_steps: Once the billing logic is live, perform a 'smoke test' by creating a test Stripe account, then move to customer acquisition by building the landing page copy using the same value metrics you defined here.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start building for scale. This framework is the bridge between a hobbyist project and a professional SaaS business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
